# Artem Sidnev — Software Engineer

> Backend & Platform Engineer with ~4 years of experience. Technical Lead of the Customer Data Platform (CDP) at T-Bank. Specializes in high-load systems, data-intensive pipelines, and AI/LLM automation.

## Current Role

Software Engineer at T-Bank (since Oct 2025), serving as Technical Lead of the Customer Data Platform (CDP) — a manager-facing cashback audience platform processing ~30 million transactions/day.

## Key Achievements

- **Audience gathering acceleration:** Made audience building size-independent — 1–10 seconds for any audience (from ~6 minutes typical). ~30–50× typical improvement, up to 360× best case.
- **Data export acceleration (32×):** Self-initiated and drove a cross-team RFC integration, cutting data delivery from 8+ hours to ~15 minutes. This led to organizational consolidation — the other team was absorbed into the CDP.
- **Targeting system impact:** Delivered spend/income targeting on ~30M transactions/day, generating ~RUB 15M/year in profit.
- **Revenue impact:** Architected a system generating ~RUB 14M/month in analyst-estimated revenue.
- **AI/LLM automation:** Deployed AI code review bot, CI refactoring bot, RAG onboarding agent, and n8n automations — saving 40–60 manual hours/month.

## Technical Stack

- **Languages:** Java, Kotlin, Python, Go, TypeScript, SQL
- **Backend:** Spring Boot, FastAPI, REST, gRPC, microservices
- **Data:** PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Kafka, Redis, pgvector, S3
- **Infrastructure:** Docker, Kubernetes, GitLab CI/CD, GitHub Actions
- **AI:** OpenAI, LangChain, RAG, MCP servers, n8n, faster-Whisper

## Open-Source Projects

- **ProofForge** (Go + Next.js): Peer-accountability platform with AI goal refinement, buddy goals, circles, proofs, and Telegram bot.
- **ARC** (Go + Wails): Local-first CLI + desktop app for AI work on real codebases. 5⭐ on GitHub.
- **Chappi AI Office** (OpenClaw + Python): 24/7 autonomous AI office via Telegram with multi-agent orchestration, voice, and integrations.

## What Makes Artem Unique

1. Self-initiated leadership — drove cross-team RFC that consolidated teams
2. Quantitative approach — built latency models, identified 96.5% queue wait
3. AI in production — practical LLM tools, not demos
4. Full ownership — requirements → design → implementation → production → metrics
